plan_metadata.name: Feature LocalDebug Custom API without OpenAI Keys

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" popup within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, selecting the template for creating agents, bots, web pages, or apps for Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Teams Agent with Data" dropdown option in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code, selecting the agent type for data-driven interactions.

Click the "Customize" option in the dropdown menu under "Select an option to load your data" in the "Teams Agent with Data" window of Visual Studio Code. The crosshair is precisely positioned on the highlighted "Customize" menu item.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" input field in the OpenAPI Description Document dialog within Visual Studio Code.

Type the URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SLdragon/example-openapi-spec/main/real-no-auth.yaml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface within Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input of the OpenAPI specification URL in the address bar within Visual Studio Code's "OpenAPI Description Document" interface.
Click the "GET /repairs" option in the operation selection dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the language selection button (displaying a flag icon) in the API operation dropdown at the top-right of the Visual Studio Code window to change the language for the selected API operation.
Click the "OpenAI" dropdown option under "Select a service to access LLMs" in the "Service for Large Language Model (LLM)" pop-up of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit within VS Code.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input in the "Input Azure OpenAI service key now or set it later in the project" text box in the Azure OpenAI Key prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default Folder" input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog of Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: python

```python
import os

file_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_DNmrJ/src/app.py"

# Verify file exists
if not os.path.isfile(file_path):
    print(f"File does not exist: {file_path}")
    exit(1)

with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Replace only the first occurrence
new_content = content.replace("model=config.OPENAI_MODEL_NAME", "model=config.OPENAI_MODEL_NAME, azure_endpoint = \"\"", 1)

with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(new_content)

print("Replacement complete.")
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Replacement complete.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code replace "model=config.OPENAI_MODEL_NAME" with "model=config.OPENAI_MODEL_NAME, azure_endpoint = """ in file \"/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/src/app.py\""
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code interface with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active, to open the command palette for agent development tasks.
Type text: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the Visual Studio Code command palette (top-center search box) within the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground extension workspace.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion  'OpenAI Key' is visible
Type text: 'fackedkey' into the OpenAI Key input field in the Visual Studio Code extension prompt, confirming the entry for API authentication.
Press 'Enter' to confirm the Azure OpenAI Key entry in the password input dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Open" button in the app details popup for vscuse_app_4YhGglocal within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Open" button in the vsсuse_app_4YhGglocal app onboarding dialog within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
